title: AgentRun.Umbraco Workflow Definition
description: Schema for AgentRun.Umbraco workflow.yaml files. Mirrors the runtime WorkflowValidator — anything this schema accepts must be accepted by the engine, and anything the engine rejects must be rejected here. Reference this schema from workflow.yaml via a '# yaml-language-server: $schema=...' directive for editor autocomplete and inline validation.

name.description: Human-readable name of the workflow shown in the AgentRun dashboard.

description.description: Short description of what this workflow does. Shown in the workflow list and first-run UX.

mode.description: Execution mode. 'interactive' pauses between steps for user input; 'autonomous' runs steps back-to-back to completion. Optional — defaults to 'interactive' when omitted.

default_profile.description: Default Umbraco.AI profile alias used for steps that do not specify their own 'profile'.

icon.description: Optional icon identifier used by the dashboard when rendering this workflow in lists.

variants.description: Reserved for future workflow variant support. The runtime validator permits this key with free-form contents today — do not rely on a stable inner shape yet.

config.description: Optional flat map of workflow-level variable values exposed to agent prompts as {key} tokens at prompt-assembly time. Keys must match [a-z0-9_]+; values must be strings. Runtime-provided variables ({today}, {instance_id}, {step_index}, {previous_artifacts}) take precedence on key collision. An explicit null (config:) is equivalent to omitting the key. See Story 11.7.

tool_defaults.description: Workflow-level tool tuning. Values declared here apply to every step unless a step-level 'tool_overrides' block overrides them. Resolution chain: step tool_overrides → workflow tool_defaults → site AgentRun:ToolLimits defaults → engine default, all capped by the site-level ceiling. See Story 9.6 (fetch_url / tool_loop) and Story 9.9 (read_file).

steps.description: Ordered list of workflow steps. At least one step is required.

id.description: Unique step identifier within this workflow (snake_case recommended). Used in artifact paths and the step-progress sidebar.

name.description: Human-readable step name shown in the dashboard step sidebar.

description.description: Optional short description of what this step does. Surfaced in the dashboard when available.

agent.description: Relative path (from the workflow.yaml folder) to the agent markdown file that drives this step.

profile.description: Umbraco.AI profile alias override for this step. Falls back to the workflow 'default_profile' when omitted.

tools.description: Tool names this step is allowed to call (e.g. fetch_url, read_file, write_file). Must match tools registered with the engine.

reads_from.description: Artifact file paths (relative to the instance artifacts folder) this step reads. Shown on the dashboard so users can see step dependencies.

writes_to.description: Artifact file paths this step is expected to produce. Used by the artifact browser and the completion checker.

data_files.description: Additional read-only data files (relative to the workflow folder) made available to this step at prompt-assembly time.

completion_check.description: Criteria the engine uses to decide whether this step is complete.

files_exist.description: Non-empty list of artifact files that must exist for the step to be considered complete.

tool_overrides.description: Step-level tool tuning. Values declared here override 'tool_defaults' for this step only. See the resolution chain on 'tool_defaults'.

toolTuning.description: Tool tuning block. Only the explicitly supported tools and settings are allowed — unknown tools or settings are rejected by the runtime validator.

max_response_bytes.description: Maximum response size in bytes for a single fetch_url call. Engine default 1048576 (1 MB). Resolution chain: step tool_overrides → workflow tool_defaults → site AgentRun:ToolLimits:FetchUrl:MaxResponseBytesDefault → engine default, capped by AgentRun:ToolLimits:FetchUrl:MaxResponseBytesCeiling. Introduced in Story 9.6.

timeout_seconds.description: Per-request timeout in seconds for fetch_url. Engine default 10. Resolution chain: step tool_overrides → workflow tool_defaults → site AgentRun:ToolLimits:FetchUrl:TimeoutSecondsDefault → engine default, capped by AgentRun:ToolLimits:FetchUrl:TimeoutSecondsCeiling. Introduced in Story 9.6.

max_response_bytes.description: Maximum bytes returned from a single read_file call (defence-in-depth against oversized artifacts). Engine default 1048576 (1 MB). Resolution chain: step tool_overrides → workflow tool_defaults → site AgentRun:ToolLimits:ReadFile:MaxResponseBytesDefault → engine default, capped by AgentRun:ToolLimits:ReadFile:MaxResponseBytesCeiling. Introduced in Story 9.9.

user_message_timeout_seconds.description: How long the tool loop waits for a user message in interactive mode before giving up. Engine default 300. Resolution chain: step tool_overrides → workflow tool_defaults → site AgentRun:ToolLimits:ToolLoop:UserMessageTimeoutSecondsDefault → engine default, capped by AgentRun:ToolLimits:ToolLoop:UserMessageTimeoutSecondsCeiling. Introduced in Story 9.6.
